title: Bytes, Text I/O, Encodings, Newlines, and Cleanup · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for bytes, text i/o, encodings, newlines, and cleanup and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, terminal, packaging, operating-system, load, security, and release transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for explicit text-I/O boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish raw bytes, decoded str values, code points, grapheme clusters, and serialized output at every boundary.
Choose and document UTF-8 or another declared encoding instead of relying on locale-dependent defaults.
Select strict rejection, replacement, ignore, or surrogate handling from the stakeholder evidence duty and expose data loss.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Bytes Text Boundary · changed case · Quiz Bytes Text Boundary
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Bookbot team handles case 8527 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to decode bytes under an explicit UTF-8 and cleanup contract without hiding malformed input; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. options.text: For diagnose the hidden failure, run one convenient ASCII text, omit corpus authority, repository and policy identity, bytes, encoding, Unicode, offsets, reconciliation, ties, stderr, status, changed cases, tests, recovery, and native transfer, then accept a plausible report as Bookbot proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Context Cleanup
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Context Cleanup: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Bookbot team handles case 1860 during a digital-humanities corpus revision. Build deterministic Python evidence to decode bytes under an explicit UTF-8 and cleanup contract without hiding malformed input; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use context management and narrow exception scopes so handles close and the causal error remains observable.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Context Cleanup.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Context Cleanup, keep Context Cleanup connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use context management and narrow exception scopes so handles close and the causal error remains observable.
